programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy needs to find a way to help a distressed cats uncontrollable spraying. He also tries to persuade an owner to stop feeding her diabetic cat lasagne

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy deals with a bullying cat who is holding all the female felines hostage, causing them to hide in the bathroom. He also meets a therapy cat working for a charity

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: An injured deer is found hiding in a shed after being attacked. The game wardens pull over a suspicious vehicle and notice the driver is hiding something under his seat

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet

programme.desc: Pete Nelson and his team build a butterfly-themed treehouse in a cluster of six trees, featuring a climbing net and a loft, for the residents of a therapeutic centre

programme.desc: Officer Sadler battles to save a dogs life after discovering two others that were left to starve to death, and a woman loses her cats following a fire at her apartment

programme.title: Meet the Sloths

programme.desc: The sanctuary team members work to save a poisoned sloth. They also try to find a much-loved toy, and the resident scientist attempts to pin down the secret of sloth manliness

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Chicago Bulls star Jimmy Butler calls Wayde and Brett to build him an old-school boombox aquarium that lights up and plays music

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: As Hurricane Rita destroyed huge areas of Texas, the SPCA officers worked around the clock to organise a huge animal rescue operation

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: When Barney, a dog with an embedded collar, is spotted in the Houston countryside Investigator Kristi Franks is called into action and a desperate chase ensues

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: A phone call in the middle of the night sees Tia racing to deal with a family emergency. A homeless man reaches out for help when his dog has trouble breathing

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy is in Philadelphia to help a cat named Charlee rebuild the confidence that her feline sister Frankie has nearly beaten out of her

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy meets an outdoor cat that has been forced to live inside - and has protested by soaking the place in urine

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Game Warden Eddins assists the local police with an animal cruelty case. Richards responds to a trespassing dispute and Roraback relocates a snapping turtle

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.sub-title: The Heart of a Pit Bull
programme.desc: Dr Jeff and his staff treat a pet goat that has been injured in a freak accident and a dog that has been unwell ever since swallowing a mysterious object

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.sub-title: Never Too Old for a Treehouse
programme.desc: Pete Nelson heads to Oregon to fulfil one of the greatest wishes of 81-year-old client Marlene, who has always wanted a luxury treehouse

programme.title: Gator Boys
programme.desc: Jimmy suggests an expansion of the rescue operation, and ends up clashing with Paul. However, when the volunteers begin to take sides in the dispute, the pair must put aside their differences to save their venture from ruin

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: The Kilchers are preparing for winter, as Eivin scales treacherous peaks to fill his freezer and Otto rushes to help when Shanes cabin build takes a perilous turn

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Neil is on the brink of reopening the Motherlode mine for tourism, but a new arrival puts his empire at risk. Jeremy begins a new venture to grow his family farm

programme.title: Beasts of the Bayou
programme.desc: Fishermen search for the half-man, half-alligator creature known as the Parlangua, which is thought to be responsible for a spate of recent attacks

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: A pair of pig-farmers in Virginia seek help stopping predators from killing their livestock, forcing Marty Raney and his daughter Misty to suggest they abandon the free-range dream

programme.title: Dog Detectives
programme.desc: PC Bartley reminisces about his first dog, who was killed in action chasing a gang of burglars. Sniffer dog Lucy is called into action when a driver is stopped

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Out on a Limb
programme.desc: An arborist pushes Pete Nelson and his team to the limit by requesting a tri-level residence 60 feet above the ground in three giant redwood trees

programme.title: Animal Cops San Francisco
programme.desc: The officers reveal their most stressful, action-packed and heart-wrenching stories from the series

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia
programme.desc: The team receives a midnight call-out and discovers hundreds of mistreated animals, including more than 200 birds, dogs and roosters

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming concludes his journey by travelling from Barton Swing Aqueduct to Liverpool Docks - but he experiences engine issues and a leaky boat along the way

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Industrial Age
programme.desc: Fred visits the North East, a region rich in transport history, to explore the development of Britains railways, from the first steam locomotives to the record-breaking Mallard. A visit to Yorks National Railway Museum offers the chance to ride a replica of Stephensons Rocket, while at Ffestiniog Railway he learns about the computer-aided design process used for new locomotives

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Industrial Age
programme.desc: A tour of Britains mining heritage takes Fred from Cornwall to Scotland, where he visits sites of lead, slate, tin and coal extraction. Along the way, the machine-mad former steeplejack has a chance to man a winding engine and descends a mine shaft while strapped to the top of the cage

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming travels along the Rochdale Canal from Sowerby Bridge to Manchester. But disaster strikes when he runs aground, threatening to stop him from reaching his final destination

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against guests Annette Page, Richard Baker and Robin Ray. With guest musician Murray Perahia. First broadcast in 1979

programme.title: The Secret Life of Waves
programme.desc: David Malone investigates the importance of ocean waves. He discovers they are not made of water, can travel sideways and their sound is created by bubbles, and examines the connection between the life cycles of waves and human beings

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Industrial Age
programme.desc: Fred visits the North East, a region rich in transport history, to explore the development of Britains railways, from the first steam locomotives to the record-breaking Mallard. A visit to Yorks National Railway Museum offers the chance to ride a replica of Stephensons Rocket, while at Ffestiniog Railway he learns about the computer-aided design process used for new locomotives

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming concludes his journey by travelling from Barton Swing Aqueduct to Liverpool Docks - but he experiences engine issues and a leaky boat along the way

programme.title: What Are UFOs?
programme.desc: For decades, UFOs have intrigued the world, but many scientists avoided studying them. In the US, after highly publicised sightings of unidentified objects by US Navy pilots, UFOs are moving out of the shadows and into the light as NASA pledges to study them scientifically. This programme follows astrophysicists and engineers using new technologies to investigate strange objects in the sky

programme.title: Talking Art
programme.desc: Celebrities discuss their favourite works by Cezanne, including Raymond Blanc who uses his culinary knowledge to comment on Still Life. First broadcast October 20, 2000

programme.title: Building Britains Biggest Nuclear Power Station
programme.desc: Part two of two. Following the challenges involved with building the Hinkley Point C in the remote Somerset countryside, the first new nuclear power station in Britain for a generation. This time, the team creates an extensive flood defence system to protect the site from a nuclear disaster like the one that befell the Fukushima Daiichi power plant in Japan. Elsewhere, cameras follow specialists as they work to uncover unexploded Second World War bombs that threaten the installation of the 4500-tonne cooling water intake heads

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Warrens new-found lover calls it quits in a dramatic turn of events, while Eggs father provides a constant reminder of his own inadequacies. Only Anna sees through Jerrys self-pity and tells him to buck up his ideas - with surprising results. Drama, starring Jason Hughes, Paul Copley and Daniela Nardini

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Egg gets a job delivering phone books, but doesnt have a car and finds himself forced to use a pram instead. Warren wallows in gloom and Miles deals with a man accused of revealing rather more of himself than decency permits. Andrew Lincoln, Jason Hughes and Jack Davenport star

programme.title: Catching Historys Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston explores the forensic advances that have elevated murder weapons from mere instruments to crucial pieces of evidence. She sees how arsenic, the undetectable weapon of choice in the 19th century, was exposed as the killer thanks to one simple chemical test, and finds out how distinctive marks on a victims skull led to the weapon and, in turn, the killer. Gabriel also looks at the latest forensic developments, from 3-D laser scanning that can reconstruct the sequence of events at the scene of a gun crime to the pioneering chemistry able to determine where in the world someone has been based on just a few strands of their hair

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Industrial Age
programme.desc: The former steeplejack recalls the golden age of shipbuilding, examining the skills of engineers who turned Britain into one of the leading manufacturing nations, and visits Bristol to pay tribute to his personal hero Isambard Kingdom Brunel. He also travels to Scotland for a voyage on the paddle steamer Waverley

programme.title: Building Britains Biggest Nuclear Power Station
programme.desc: Part two of two. Following the challenges involved with building the Hinkley Point C in the remote Somerset countryside, the first new nuclear power station in Britain for a generation. This time, the team creates an extensive flood defence system to protect the site from a nuclear disaster like the one that befell the Fukushima Daiichi power plant in Japan. Elsewhere, cameras follow specialists as they work to uncover unexploded Second World War bombs that threaten the installation of the 4500-tonne cooling water intake heads

programme.title: Catching Historys Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston explores the forensic advances that have elevated murder weapons from mere instruments to crucial pieces of evidence. She sees how arsenic, the undetectable weapon of choice in the 19th century, was exposed as the killer thanks to one simple chemical test, and finds out how distinctive marks on a victims skull led to the weapon and, in turn, the killer. Gabriel also looks at the latest forensic developments, from 3-D laser scanning that can reconstruct the sequence of events at the scene of a gun crime to the pioneering chemistry able to determine where in the world someone has been based on just a few strands of their hair

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Industrial Age
programme.desc: The former steeplejack recalls the golden age of shipbuilding, examining the skills of engineers who turned Britain into one of the leading manufacturing nations, and visits Bristol to pay tribute to his personal hero Isambard Kingdom Brunel. He also travels to Scotland for a voyage on the paddle steamer Waverley

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the hidden villages of London. Despite many rural settlements like Hornsey and Dagenham being swallowed up by the expansion of London, Hampstead residents successfully fought to preserve their village heritage, while in recent years Londoners have created a new breed of urban villages like Crouch End and Walthamstow

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank and the Family That Built Gothic Britain
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the changing fortunes of a family of architects, following the rise and fall of George Gilbert Scott, his son George Junior, and grandson Giles. Presenter Dan Cruickshank finds out how the dynasty was responsible for the creation of iconic landmarks such as the Midland Hotel at St Pancras station, and helped shape the Victorian age with their designs

programme.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly (1966)
programme.desc: A laconic gunslinger, a ruthless hired killer and a conniving outlaw hunt for a cache of loot during the chaos of the American Civil War. However, each is privy to only part of the information necessary to find it, setting in motion a series of deadly betrayals. The third film in Sergio Leones Spaghetti Western trilogy, starring Clint Eastwood as the Man with No Name, with Lee Van Cleef and Eli Wallach. Featuring Ennio Morricones iconic soundtrack

programme.title: Clint Eastwood: The Man with No Name
programme.desc: A profile of the American actor and film director in which he talks to Iain Johnstone, with Richard Burton, Sergio Leone, Don Siegel, Pauline Kael, Dilys Powell. There is extracts from some of Eastwoods films, including Rawhide, The Good, the Bad and the Ugly, Where Eagles Dare, The Outlaw Josey Wales and The Enforcer. First broadcast 23 February 1977

programme.title: Word Up! Black American Pop at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of BBC performances by African-American artists of the 1980s, featuring Salt-N-Pepa, Cameo, Luther Vandross, Doug E Fresh, Whitney Houston, Chaka Khan, Shalamar and Kid Creole

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the hidden villages of London. Despite many rural settlements like Hornsey and Dagenham being swallowed up by the expansion of London, Hampstead residents successfully fought to preserve their village heritage, while in recent years Londoners have created a new breed of urban villages like Crouch End and Walthamstow

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank and the Family That Built Gothic Britain
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the changing fortunes of a family of architects, following the rise and fall of George Gilbert Scott, his son George Junior, and grandson Giles. Presenter Dan Cruickshank finds out how the dynasty was responsible for the creation of iconic landmarks such as the Midland Hotel at St Pancras station, and helped shape the Victorian age with their designs

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the comedy quiz show in which a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win some brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he is joined by Richard Ayoade, Alex Jones, Stephen Bailey, Nadia Jae, Gyles Brandreth and Roisin Conaty

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Police Target Badger Crime and Quad Bike Mayhem
programme.desc: The RNLI and Coastguard launch a rescue operation to save 12 sheep that are stranded on rocks in Strangford Lough. In North Wales, a wildlife photographer captures evidence of a badger sett under attack and reporter Paul Colgan joins the police Quad Squad in South Yorkshire as they tackle illegal off-road riding

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell is struck by the fetching colour of a County Durham house, while Dion Dublin is impressed by whats on offer in Greater Manchester and Derbyshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian explores the Museum of Leathercraft in Northampton, while David Harper and Kate Bliss help the teams to find the perfect purchase in the historic village of Naseby

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: Joanna Page is in the market town of Carmarthen, where friends Joy and Thembi, both from Swansea, hunt for charity shop bargains to raise money for Womens Aid. Armed with £100 each and 60 minutes on the clock, they are paired with expert thrifters Jen and Mirain in their search of resale gold

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Banjo Beale and his team travel to Skye to transform a dated cafe and bookshop, and the interior designer takes the bold decision to spend almost all his budget on a set of huge stained-glass windows. Far from home and with tricky logistics, will this job push the team to the limit and will Banjos ambitious gamble pay off with his clients?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Richie Anderson makes his debut on the show, helping a couple from Birmingham to find a new home in the rural Midlands, as well as taking a ride on a narrowboat

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.sub-title: Art and Digital Illustration and Woodturning
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea revisits digital artist Tasia and woodturner Shannon to hear about their success. Retail worker Tasia dreamt of becoming a full-time artist, while Shannon reveals how broadening her business is helping her to build a long-term creative career

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Tom Allen host the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and JB Gill present the magazine show, with celebrity guests and stories of interest from around the UK

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Paranoia takes it grip on Vicki. Priya continues to worry about Ravi and Oscar puts Lauren and Max to the test

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Surrey man Bobby Williams brings in the powered wheelchair that opened up a whole new sporting career for him as a youngster, allowing him to play the fiercely competitive game of powerchair football. Now moving into coaching, he wants to pass the chair on to a new player - but it needs restoration. The other items are a speedway flag that tells a remarkable Glaswegian love story, meticulously handcrafted jewellery and a bagatelle board

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of the ambulance service across Yorkshire

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Champions League
programme.desc: Action from the quarter-final second-leg ties, including Arsenal v Sporting Lisbon and Emirates Stadium and Liverpool v Paris Saint-Germain at Anfield. The two remaining English sides will have been hoping to make home advantage count and progress to the last four, but Liverpool faced a particularly tough test against the holders. Plus, Bayern Munich v Real Madrid and Atletico Madrid v Barcelona

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents a celebrity edition of the high stakes quiz, with a team of athletes featuring Kriss Akabusi, Sammi Kinghorn, Matt Baker and Gladiator Karenjeet Kaur-Bains playing for charity. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: A panel of BBC journalists hosts round-up from Westminster, delivering the usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: RAF Saves Trapped Cow and Police Catch Poaching Suspect
programme.desc: Police uncover shocking evidence of animal cruelty linked to a series of farmland poaching attacks in Worksop, Nottinghamshire, and the RAF tackle a challenging rescue when Beauty the cow becomes trapped at the bottom of a steep gorge in Gilsland, Cumbria. Paul Colgan joins a rural crime officer using forensic DNA technology to mark valuable machinery in Ellerton, East Yorkshire

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin has to navigate his way through an eight-bedroom behemoth in Bangor. Jacqui Joseph takes in the views in Dover, and Tommy Walsh looks around a bungalow in Lincolnshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In a special show, Eric Knowles celebrates the glamour and sophistication of the 1920s and 30s. He looks back at a host of art deco-inspired pieces bought by Bargain Hunt teams, from cocktail sets and mirrors to Anglepoise lamps and bronze sculptures. Eric also admires the geometric architecture of the Jazz Age and visits Stockports stunning art deco cinema, as well as a unique home in Northampton designed by Charles Rennie Mackintosh

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Llandudno, competitors Anne and Lenka race to turn charity shop bargains into profit, taking bold risks on furniture, jewellery and creative upcycles, in the hope of raising money for Cancer Research Wales. Presented by Joanna Page

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Banjo Beale makes over two spaces on the Isle of Mull, turning a Tobermory castle turret room into a BandB destination and revamping a guest bedroom at the Rubha nan Gall lighthouse. Contending with complicated logistics and bad weather, Banjo and the team must go above and beyond to complete two makeovers in these stunning locations

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Williams helps a couple with four kids relocate to North Yorkshire, and meets a couple who have turned their passion for bee-keeping into a buzzing new life

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea revisits ceramic sculptor Sarah from Yorkshire and glass artist Emmy from Devon to see how their fortunes have changed. When Sarah arrived at Doms bootcamp, she was new to her craft, but thanks to his support and guidance, she has built a growing business. Last time, Emmy was returning to glassblowing after an eight-year break. Now that she is selling around the world

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A desperate Vicki finds herself out of options, and its a battle of the Brannings as Max and Lauren continue to compete

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams bid farewell to Europe and head into Asia, racing from bustling Istanbu to the remote town of Halfeti in south-east Turkey - where the last to arrive will be eliminated from the competition. Along the way, Katie persuades brother Harrison to tick off a bucket list experience - watching the hot-air balloons rise over the chimneys of Cappadocia - while the leaders in the last leg, cousins Puja and Roshni, continue to celebrate, this time because its Pujas birthday

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Its the final and as usual the remaining two candidates go head to head to win Lord Sugars investment. Supported by a handful of returning candidates, they must launch their businesses to a roomful of industry and a few familiar faces from the past 20 years, before the business mogul decides who will become his next partner

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Frances second-largest city Marseille has been the nations drug capital for over 50 years, but the violence of its drug war is escalating, as rival gangs kidnap, torture, kill and even set one another on fire. Ashley spends time in the notorious northern suburbs known as the cites, meeting young men involved in fighting the bloody drug war as well as riding along with the police on a night-time patrol

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Despite being sentenced to nearly seven years in a maximum security prison, Michaella was home in less than three. She reveals how she got out early and how the experience changed her for the better

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May try to impress Oxford residents by building an electric car, and refuse to let an unenthusiastic response deter them from sitting in on the machines crash-test before handing it over to the Stigs environmentally friendly cousin. Actor Michael Sheen is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, and Jeremy pits the Corvette ZR1 against the Audi R8 V10

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sketch comedy following the residents of a quiet suburban street that quickly becomes a playground for comic mayhem-absurd, awkward, and unexpectedly hilarious

programme.title: Louis Therouxs LA Stories
programme.desc: The intrepid reporter explores different aspects of Los Angeles, beginning by meeting the characters whose lives revolve around the citys neglected and feral dogs. He goes on patrol with a man on a mission to rescue some of the thousands of stray and ferocious pit bulls roaming the streets and meets hard-pressed staff at LAs largest animal shelter. Louis also encounters training gurus who claim to be able to take the ghetto out of the dog once it has been removed from that environment

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Florida to track down Americas largest snake, catch a slippery amphibian called the amphiuma and witness the hunting methods of bottlenose dolphins

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall reveals the advanced camera technology and tricks of the trade that allow the Deadly 60 team to catch dramatic wildlife shots, including the breathtaking breach attack of a great white shark

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond debate which company has made the greatest number of exceptional vehicles, and James May finds a way of transporting caravans to campsites without inconveniencing other road users. The Top Gear track plays host to a replica of one of the worlds most famous rally cars, and broadcaster Chris Evans is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Its week 11, and the final five candidates face a gruelling round of interviews with some of Lord Sugars toughest-talking business associates. As their business plans are picked apart, some contestants hold their own, while others crumble under the pressure. In the boardroom, Alan Sugar has a tough decision to make, as only two can make it through to next weeks grand final

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: The former footballer visits some of the worlds most dangerous cities to explore the issues that affect young men born into a life on the fringes of society. He begins in the favelas of Rio, Brazil, where he learns what it takes to become a lookout, foot soldier and drug dealer in a world where death could become reality at any moment. He also comes face-to-face with a gun-toting militia boss to uncover how these men rule through fear and intimidation

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: South Africa has for years battled the scourge of rhino poaching, fuelled by insatiable demand for their horns in Asia. Ashley embarks on a mission to find out who is behind these acts, discovering how the challenges young men face growing up in deprived rural communities can lead them into poaching

programme.title: Doctor Who: Unleashed
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the Time Lords adventures in the 60th anniversary specials

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond debate which company has made the greatest number of exceptional vehicles, and James May finds a way of transporting caravans to campsites without inconveniencing other road users. The Top Gear track plays host to a replica of one of the worlds most famous rally cars, and broadcaster Chris Evans is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part one of two. Tir Dhondy reveals the realities behind modern Las Vegas. The first episode explores the risk of crime faced by tourists and visits a pool party where massive screens show scores of live sports events from around the world so that partygoers can bet from the comfort of their sun lounger

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Short film about four very different schoolgirls thrown together as they plan their escape from detention. Written by and starring Sabrina Ali

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: When a teenage boy has a girl over for the first time, the overly attached monster under his bed has a meltdown. Comedy short film, starring Freddie Meredith

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Brothers Steve and Stu scramble to put on a last-minute birthday show for Steves daughter. If they fail, Steves in the doghouse and Stu might end up dead. Comic short film, written by and starring Stuart McPherson and Stephen Buchanan

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated comedy set in 1990s east London. Joyces birthday present has been stolen, so her husband enlists the help of his fearsome mother-in-law to track it down

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sketch show set in Belfast, where the cats get stoned, the pigeons get philosophical and Gambo forgets how to breathe

programme.title: Louis Therouxs LA Stories
programme.desc: California has some of the toughest regulations controlling sex offenders anywhere in America - after leaving prison they can spend years on parole being tagged and tracked and a lifetime on an online register. In his final film exploring different aspects of life in Los Angeles, Louis Theroux visits hostels to talk to parolees as well as the people charged with monitoring them, and asks whether the restrictions keep society safer and when, if ever, it is right to give sex offenders a second chance

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Donna Nobles past catches up with her, as she finds herself reunited with the time traveller. The first of three editions originally broadcast for the shows 60th anniversary, starring David Tennant as the 14th Doctor and Catherine Tate as companion Donna Noble

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: An out-of-control Tardis takes the Doctor and Donna to the furthest edge of adventure, to face the most desperate fight of their lives, with the fate of the universe at stake

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The giggle of a mysterious puppet is driving the human race insane. When the Doctor discovers the return of an old enemy, he faces a fight he can never win. Neil Patrick Harris guest stars as 1960s villain the Toymaker and Ncuti Gatwa makes his debut as the 15th Doctor

programme.title: Talking Doctor Who
programme.desc: David Tennant time travels back through the BBC archive to tell the story of the Doctors classic era, in a programme originally shown as part of the 60th anniversary celebrations. The selection features rarely seen interviews and clips covering William Hartnells first incarnation right through to the seventh Doctor, Sylvester McCoy, and David compares his experiences with those who came before him

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas

The selection features rarely seen interviews and clips covering William Hartnells first incarnation right through to the seventh Doctor, Sylvester McCoy, and David compares his experiences with those who came before him

programme.title: Nowhere Special (2020)
programme.desc: When John, a 35-year-old window cleaner, is given only a few months to live, he attempts to find a new, perfect family for his three-year-old son, determined to shield him from the terrible reality of the situation. Drama, starring James Norton, Daniel Lamont and Eileen OHiggins

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: As Lord Sugar continues his search for a new business partner, Angela Scanlon and her guests take a deep dive into the candidates performances in the interview stage of the competition, before meeting the three candidates who didnt make the cut

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: The lads decide its time to expand their empire in Cork city, as their days of doing all the hard work are over - they plan on being the bosses now. Starring Alex Murphy and Hilary Rose

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Leaving the tranquillity of Kefalonia behind, the teams race through Greece to reach one of Europes biggest and most vibrant cities, Istanbul, where Europe and Asia collide. As ever, money is tight and just one missed connection can mean disaster. Without mobile phones to help them, the teams have to rely on their wits to make it to the end of the second stage. John Hannah narrates

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts encounters a layout he has never seen before, which is rare given his 20-plus years on the show. Dion Dublin encounters all weathers in Manchester and Leeds

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Richie Anderson helps a couple fulfil their forest school dream in Oxfordshire. He also meets an innovative artist who creates masterpieces on a bicycle-powered sewing machine

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: South West England: Starter and Fish
programme.desc: Four chefs from south west England cook canapes, starters and fish dishes and their creations are judged by Michelin-starred chef Simon Rogan. Dishes include a potato peel pie that pays homage to the film The Guernsey Literary and Potato Peel Pie Society. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clive Myries Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: Clive travels north to the Goldeneye estate, which was once the home of James Bond writer Ian Fleming. This is the creative birthplace of 007, and all 14 of the Bond books were written here. Clive props up the bar at Flemings favourite watering hole and enjoys a martini, dives into the water with a group of former fishermen who are saving the islands coral reef, samples some mouth-watering Rastafarian Ital cuisine, and visits a legalised cannabis farm

programme.title: Ancient Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Michael Scott employs the latest 3D-scanning technology and virtual reality to reveal the historical secrets of Cairo and Ancient Egypt. He explores the first pyramid ever built at Saqqara and finds out how it helped inspire the Great Pyramid of Giza. With access to robbers tunnels and hidden chambers normally closed to the public, the presenter uses laser scans to help understand how the pyramids were built and shows how the Great Pyramid was designed and constructed. Michael then investigates the Sphinx and tries to determine which pharaoh is represented in the face of the legendary monument

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents from Bridgend in south Wales, where a team of experts offers professional opinions on items including a Victorian hat, some locally made plates, a whales tooth, a bow-fronted barometer and a bargain-priced pot with a surprising history

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Barrow Hill Railway Heritage Centre in Derbyshire sees hundreds of visitors arrive with their family heirlooms as the shows experts find some unusual items to try out at auction. Presenter Paul Martin also visits Haversage and finds out about its connection with Charlotte Brontes book Jane Eyre

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph, Ben Thompson, Laura Tobin and Henning Wehn take on a series of quiz rounds selected to test their general knowledge skills

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo takes a luxury, panoramic Shimikaze train to the Shima peninsula. It transports visitors to Ise Jingu, Japans most sacred Shinto shrine. The next stop is Nara, the ancient capital of Japan, a tranquil city where some 1,400 sika deer roam the green open space. He also visits the Buddhist Todaiji Temple, where he sees one of the largest bronze Buddha statues in the world

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: South West England: Main and Dessert
programme.desc: The remaining three chefs from south west England cook mains and desserts and creations are judged by veteran judge Simon Rogan. They include a guinea fowl dish designed to bring Aardman character Feathers McGraw to life, as well as a rum-soaked savarin, earl grey tea mousse and a milk ice cream paying homage to Sense and Sensibility

programme.title: Artemis: To the Moon and Back
programme.desc: The inside story of Nasas mission around the Moon and back, with astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen having gones deeper into space than anyone has gone before. For the past three-and-a-half years, this Horizon special has had privileged access inside Nasa to chronicle the mission, hearing from astronauts, engineers, technicians and mission directors about what it takes to mount a moonshot in the 21st century. There are further insights from some of the biggest names in space science including Maggie Aderin and British astronaut Tim peake

programme.title: Michael Jackson: An American Tragedy
programme.desc: A look at the accusations of child sexual abuse made against Michael Jackson by a 13-year-old boy. Detectives describe the shock of investigating the worlds most famous performer, while inside Michaels camp, aides recall disbelief and a frantic effort to keep Michaels worldwide tour, business empire and public image intact

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan completes this leg of his journey along Irelands Wild Atlantic in County Derry/Londonderry. He walks the 400-year-old walls of the city of Derry/Londonderry with comedian Diona Doherty. He also hits the farm to find out what it takes to be a competitive sheep herder, before heading for the windswept coastline of Castlerock to meet a skilled craft worker who builds traditional coracle boats by hand

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events, with Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: Ian and the team deal with a newly published report from the United Nations Health Agency which concludes that some key Twenty Twenty Six host cities may now be too hot to safely play football in due to global heating. The challenge is how to get ahead of the narrative so that the scheduled matches can still go ahead

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Kiri Pritchard-McLean shares her light-hearted views on modern life in this show recorded before a packed house at Londons Hammersmith Apollo. She also introduces comedians Catherine Bohart, and Babatunde Aleshe to the stage

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: South West England: Main and Dessert
programme.desc: The remaining three chefs from south west England cook mains and desserts and creations are judged by veteran judge Simon Rogan. They include a guinea fowl dish designed to bring Aardman character Feathers McGraw to life, as well as a rum-soaked savarin, earl grey tea mousse and a milk ice cream paying homage to Sense and Sensibility

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: When the team attend a Dark Morris Dancing event, Margo finds herself at the centre of a crime when a member of her team collapses during their performance. With a severe allergic reaction to blame, and the victims epi-pen case empty, Humphrey is stumped as to how the attack was triggered unseen while all eyes were on the stage. Death in Paradise spin-off, starring Kris Marshall and Felicity Montagu

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: Diversitys Ashley Banjo, actresses Hermione Norris and Patsy Kensit., reality star Tasha Ghouri; stand-up comedians Hasan Al-Habib and Ashley Blaker and broadcaster Jayne Middlemiss make a journey through the landscapes of north-east England to Lindisfarne, one of Britains most important pilgrimage sites. They trek through the heart of Northumberland and the Scottish Borders along a curated network of trails celebrating three of Englands most revered seventh-century northern English saints - Hild, Oswald and Cuthbert

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell is struck by the fetching colour of a County Durham house, while Dion Dublin is impressed by whats on offer in Greater Manchester and Derbyshire

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple who have never lived in an older home, but who are now ready to fall for a bit of period charm in either Norfolk or Suffolk. With a £270,000 budget and three lively whippets to please, they are after a dog-friendly garden and a slice of East Anglian architectural history to call home. Alistair also meets a willow farmer and discovers how this wonderfully sustainable crop is being woven into the future

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Frances Tophill returns to Damson Farm, and shares research from the RSPB on how to support garden birds. She also reveals how to make the most of sowing older seeds, and gets hands-on preparing vibrant container displays ready for the sunny days ahead. Toby Buckland explains how to make the most of outdoor spaces. And Joe Swift travels to the Cornish coast to meet a gardener who proves that even the wildest seaside weather cant stop a true planting paradise

programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clive Myries Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: On the Western tip of Jamaica Clive meets up with cousins he hasnt seen in decades. In the kitchen, he serves up a classic island dish of curry goat for all the guests. Clive also learns what it takes to harvest Jamaican honey, tries his hand at some unique pottery and makes an emotional return to the primary school his mother taught in before she emigrated to Britain in 1962

programme.title: Ancient Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Michael Scott uses 3D scanning technology to reveal the historical secrets of ancient Athens and tell the story of how this remarkable city created the worlds first democracy. He begins his journey on the Acropolis, where, in the late sixth century BC the people of Athens overthrew a tyrant and set up a democracy, and investigates a mysterious, asymmetrical temple called the Erechtheion that sits in the shadow of the world-famous Parthenon. To the south of the city, he clambers through a silver mine where an army of slaves once toiled inside the labyrinth of narrow tunnels

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents a programme from the Victoria and Albert Museum, where the team of antiques experts value treasures including letters written by Lewis Carroll, a collection of Japanese hair decorations, and a necklace with beads worn by Egyptian mummies 4,000 years ago

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Revisiting some of the locations from the series, including Greenwich Royal Naval College, Althorp House in Northamptonshire, and Herstmonceux Castle, East Sussex

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph, Ben Thompson, Laura Tobin and Henning Wehn test their general knowledge skills in the penultimate round of trivia-based games

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo starts a journey in the coastal city of Kanazawa, where he goes behind the scenes with the cleaning detail. At Kanazawa Castle, Michael discovers the history of one of the most important and powerful samurai clans in Japanese history. To finish the day, Michael travels to the Kurobe Gorge railway, a narrow gauge mountain line which snakes along one of Japans steepest gorges

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West England competition draws to an end, with the final judging, where the two remaining contestants must prepare dishes celebrating the British film industry. Actor, comedian and Wallace and Gromit voice artist Ben Whtehead joins judges Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee, and Phil Wang to decide who will be going through to the final

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: King Charles visits Campbeltowns farmers market, where Andrew and Gayle hope to impress with their premium plants. Emma and Rob host a family wedding on their farm, but their pregnant cows could go into labour at any moment, while Callum and Zara hold a historic sheepdog trial attracting competitors from afar

programme.title: Inside Barlinnie
programme.desc: Part two of two. Long-term addict Bobby heads to rehab, Billy fights for recognition that he has really changed, while Alan is released and heads back to his old ways on the streets of Glasgow

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: McCartney: The Hunt for the Lost Bass
programme.desc: The disappearance of Paul McCartneys original Hofner bass more than 50 years ago is one of rock n rolls great mysteries and this documentary tells the story of the instrument and the quest to find it. Featuring interviews with the former Beatle, his brother Mike, musicians Klaus Voormann and Elvis Costello, and roadies, journalists and fans who embarked on a mission to return the bass to its place in music history

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Darragh MacIntyre reports on Donald Trumps hardline immigration policies, which have left an estimated 14 million undocumented immigrants are at risk of deportation and resulted in a sharp rise in arrests in US cities and protesters being shot dead on the streets of Minneapolis by federal agents

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: A group hike up Scafell Pike brings buried emotions and romantic confusion to the surface. During an unforeseen storm, Mary finds herself caught in the middle of rising tensions between Mr Hayward and Mr Ryder. As their expedition takes a turn for the worse, Mary is faced with real danger, and the events of the day leave her feeling more conflicted than ever before

programme.title: Michael Jackson: An American Tragedy
programme.desc: Charting Michael Jacksons journey from child prodigy to global icon, examining the genius, controversy, trials and tragedy that shaped and continue to divide his legacy. With unique access to friends and family including Jacksons sister, La Toya, this edition charts Michael Jacksons journey from child performer to a deeply troubled musical icon

programme.title: The UnBelievable with Dan Aykroyd

programme.desc: A Vermont woman films a Loch Ness Monster-esque creature in Americas Lake Champlain. Some fear it could be Champ, a legendary plesiosaur-like creature that has been witnessed in the spot over 100 years. Deep in Russia, an enormous explosion is captured on a cars dash cam

programme.desc: Married couple Abbie and Charlie join host Anna Richardson and feature as the first picker pair as they search for another woman to join their relationship. Plus 41 year-old actor and glamour puss Chrissie from Leeds is hoping to find husband number four

programme.desc: Brendon and Priya want to explore Priyas bisexuality, but it remains to be seen whether her steamy hot tub and sensual time in the bedroom will hit the spot. Chelsea and George disagree over how far they should have taken things in the bedroom with resident couple Kiki and Sophia

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to the upmarket quarter of Hollywood to help the owner of failing health-food restaurant Sante La Brea. As well as struggling to control his disrespectful staff, the proprietor receives a surprise visit from the police at the height of dinner service

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three pairs of partners in Lancashire take part in the dinner party competition, hoping to secure the £1,000 prize. First to host are Rosie and her husband David, who have been married for 25 years and are seriously loved-up. Next, young newlyweds Melisa and Kyle cook together for the first time to mixed reviews. The third and final evening is led by bickering couple Gary and Linda, but the duo may need to work hard if they want to be in with a chance of winning the cash prize

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien brings the fairground to Liverpool in the form of a strength-testing machine, while Henry Cole restores a childs racing car from a sideshow

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George tackles a cottage conundrum, as the previous owner has removed all the upstairs walls. The seasoned architect plans to create two more bedrooms and add a bit of privacy. Plus, he meets a couple who have plans to turn their loft into a cinema room

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: Ill Be Seeing You - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sam commissions a portrait of Diane, but the artist turns out to be so obnoxious that he forbids her to pose for him any more - an order she refuses to accept. Guest starring Christopher Lloyd

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: Ill Be Seeing You - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The pretentious Semenko begins to paint Diane, but Sam despises the artist so much that he soon orders Diane to stop posing for him

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part one of two. In the aftermath of their separation, Sam returns to his playboy ways and Diane dates her psychiatrist. Kelsey Grammer makes his first appearance as Frasier

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is shocked to discover his parents planned to divorce when he was a child, and only stayed together to help him recover from an accident. Tormented by the thought they have never really been happy together, he begins to blame himself for all the misery in their marriage

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra worries that Ray is happier at work than at home and persuades him to set up an office in the house, but things do not turn out as she planned

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra decides to have a go at writing a childrens book, but makes the mistake of asking Ray to help - little realising his competitive streak will jeopardise the entire project. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frederick wins a national spelling competition, but the other entrants parents claim Frasier helped him by silently mouthing the letters of difficult words - a view shared by the judges. Comedy, guest starring Trevor Einhorn

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Roz struggles to tell Alice her hamster is dead, while Niles and Frasier return to their childhood home and discover what they believe to be evidence of a murder

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Cam Winston and Frasier decide to suspend hostilities for the sake of their parents, who have supposedly fallen for each other - though their late-blossoming romance could just be a clever subterfuge. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: In Boston for a conference, Frasier and his family bump into many of his former Cheers cronies celebrating Cliffs retirement. Niles is overwhelmed with boredom, but Martin hits it off with Norm - an occasion that promises to cause more than a little embarrassment. Comedy, guest starring Rhea Perlman, George Wendt and John Ratzenberger

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the coastal community of Long Beach, California, to save an Italian restaurant that was opened in 1958 and now under the misguided leadership of Nino, who blamed by brother Michael and sister Carina for the eaterys failings. Their 78-year-old mother Inge desperate for Nino to turn the business around, but the feuding family members will have to solve their differences first

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien meet author and broadcaster Bob Flowerdew in Norfolk, where Henry eyes up a pinball machine from a damp shed

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The presenter and his parents explore the north of England by caravan. In the opening episode, they visit Lindisfarne, home to important monuments, heritage crafts and rare wildlife. After visiting the historic castle, the Bakers get a taste of island life at Lindisfarnes only distillery, and investigate the traditions behind its upturned herring boat huts. Janice helps out in a walled garden created by a trailblazing female horticulturist

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lee Juggurnauth helps retirees Marcia and Ron from Devon find a holiday home in Crete, Greece where they can take advantage of the climate, friendly people and beautiful beaches with a budget of £255,000

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Luxury holiday villa owners Paul and Helen host a group of tennis players and Kimmie and Matt hope for a busy summer full of bookings at their freshly finished gite renovation. On the Costa del Sol, Mike and Craig prepare for the busy season in their cafe

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: On the banks of the Lot river at Château de Cezerac, Jon and Carmen are giving their tired courtyard a full facelift. At Château de Lalacelle, everything hangs in the balance as Terry and Ashley race to complete their 401 suite restoration, but there are vast curtains and a brand-new front door still to install. Also, at Château de Pican, Jane and Rory must fully renovate their dilapidated gîte before summer

programme.title: Gordon Ramsays Secret Service

programme.title: The Copenhagen Test
programme.desc: As Alexander races against the clock, Parker discovers a secret message hiding in plain sight. Espionage thriller, starring Simu Liu, Sinclair Daniel and Melissa Barrera

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to La Grange, a suburb of Chicago, to help the owners of Cafe 36, an upmarket bistro threatening to destroy the marriage of its owners due to the scale of their financial losses. programme.title: Heatwave (2022)

However, her life takes a turn when she realises she has fallen for her bosss wife. Secrets are revealed and dark motives linking to her past come to light as she becomes tangled in a web of malice. Thriller, starring Kat Graham, Merritt Patterson, Sebastian Roche and Cardi Wong.

programme.title: Kirsties House of Craft
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp shows how to turn trash into treasure with Mexican tin art, soap recycling and a lovable tin can dog

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam discovers an intimate secret concerning Dianes romance with her new boyfriend Frasier that leads him to believe she is still madly in love with him, making his ego even bigger

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Cliff meets the woman of his dreams at the Cheers Halloween fancy dress party - but they must work out if they feel the same about each other when the masks come off. John Ratzenberger stars alongside Ted Danson

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray tries to ensure Franks 65th birthday is memorable by buying him an expensive aquarium just like the one he has always admired in a local Chinese restaurant. Unfortunately, one of the fish dies, sinking the Barone household into gloom and discord

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray reluctantly attends a high-school reunion with Debra, only to discover she had been friends with the in-crowd who made his nerdy teenage years a living hell. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray tries to intercept a strongly worded letter sent by Debra, telling Marie to stop interfering in her affairs. Comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Doris Roberts

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Seattle shrink is delighted when a station in another city begins relaying his radio show - until he struggles to compete with the popularity of the host he has replaced. Elsewhere, Niles, Daphne and Martins attempt to build a table does not go according to plan

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Daphnes birthday party sparks a series of unexpected events, and after a night of intimacy, Frasier pursues Roz to her family reunion in Wisconsin, where he pretends to be her estranged partner Roger. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Peri Gilpin

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles persuades Daphnes hard-drinking father to call on her mother in Seattle, hoping he can inspire them to start afresh. Meanwhile, Frasier and Roz reflect on their night together. Comedy, guest starring Brian Cox

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Daphne impulsively marry while holidaying in Reno, but on their return they realise Frasier, Martin and Daphnes mother will be distraught if they find out they missed the event. Starring David Hyde Pierce and Jane Leeves

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay continues his travels across America saving failing restaurants from disaster. In the first of two visits to Mill Street Bistro in Norwalk, Ohio, the chef encounters Joe Nagy, who claims he has the best menu from New York to Los Angeles. However, his servers and diners tell a different story, and his resistance to change leads to some explosive confrontations

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The lads head to Birmingham, where they fall out over a bench that Henry thinks has value

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: In Thirsk, the Bakers are given a special tour of James Herriots home by his son Jim, and Matt takes to the skies for a stunning aerial view of the county by glider. Janice meets the towns yarn-bombing guerrilla knitters and visits a stunning lavender farm with Matt. Finally, the Bakers take in what Herriot dubbed the finest view in England, at Sutton Bank

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson heads to Mayenne in France to help engineer Martin and retired gardener Mandy find their dream forever home. After holidaying in the northwest of France many times, they now want to move lock, stock and barrel, with their three motorbikes and two prize parrots! Jean shows them five great properties for their budget of £50,000; will any of them rev Martin and Mandy up enough to make an offer?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: A return to the Algarve to catch up with Cheryl and Graham, who swapped life in Surrey to run a riverside BandB. In France, Devon family Brian and Paula are on the move after closing their popular eatery, while on the Costa del Sol, a turn in fortune may be on the cards for guesthouse owner Louisa

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: At Château de Lalacelle in Normandy, Terry and Ashley race to finish their opulent 401 suite, but theres still a daunting amount left to do with guests due imminently. Ben and Billie embark on a dramatic two-bedroom apartment makeover for a Valentines Day event at Château La Grande Maison. In the Dordogne, Nick and Nicole battle to transform the façade of their dilapidated chateau into an architectural statement

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: Jon returns to his favourite sport of padel, which feels like a milestone for him, as the last time he was on a court he broke his leg. But with the garden wall still not finished and Jasmine getting called away for a job, all the work is left to Jon

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Paul Merton: Driving Amazing Trains
programme.desc: Comedian Paul Merton takes charge of some of the worlds most iconic locomotives. Here he visits Switzerland, travelling up front on the Glacier Express through some of Europes most beautiful scenery. Then Paul helps stoke a 1920s stream train through the mountains to a world-famous glacier. After driving a funicular train up to the Reichenbach Falls, where Sherlock Holmes met his fictional demise, he finishes by taking a train through the ice to the summit of the Eiger

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The contestants get active as Armando Iannucci adopts a new persona to perform a daring stunt, Kumail Nanjiani grapples with bowling pins while wearing oven gloves and Joanna Page attempts a tricky skill she hasnt tried since she was about 12. Meanwhile, a topless Joel Dommett twirls a pretty umbrella and Amy Gledhill has to cope with someone shouting about scrambled eggs. Alex Horne keeps an eye on their progress, while Taskmaster Greg Davies hands out the points - and the put-downs when it all goes wrong

programme.title: Big Mood
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Nicola Coughlan and Lydia West. After not seeing each other for a year, Maggie and Eddie are reunited as bridesmaids at a mutual friends wedding. Maggie is dead set on showing everyone how stable she is, only to be given a junior bridesmaid status by the scary maid of honour. Eddie, meanwhile, is intent on cutting ties with Maggie for good. But first, she must team up with her estranged friend to save the wedding from a very unwelcome guest

programme.title: Big Mood
programme.desc: Maggie and Eddie discover their favourite drag act are performing for one night only - and tickets are like gold dust. Thus begins an odyssey across London to get tickets, involving wigs, theatrical icons, lies and a whole lot of drama. Maggie is desperate to remind Eddie how much fun they used to have, but Eddies new friend Whitney is determined to keep Eddie focused on getting a job to fund their future plans. Comedy, starring Nicola Coughlan, Lydia West and Hannah Onslow, with a guest appearance from Rupert Everett

programme.title: TFI Friday Unplugged
programme.desc: Chris Evans brings his entertainment show back to Channel 4 in a more intimate format, featuring interviews with the biggest stars and music by the latest bands from the presenters studio at Virgin Radio UK. Tonight he is joined by Sam Ryder, Peter Capaldi and Gemma Arterton

programme.title: Sky Coppers
programme.desc: The team join the search for a woman who has left a suicide note and vanished into the night. And officers are shocked when they arrive at a house to discover a gang cruelly breeding dangerous dogs in horrific conditions

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Critically ill dialysis patient Gillian desperately needs fluids, so the East Anglian Air Ambulance crew must make a tough decision. In Canvey Island, the medics take over the breathing of motorcyclist Sol who is bleeding from his ear. In Luton, Essex and Herts Air Ambulance help 16-year-old Idris who has a head and leg injury after a collision with a car. In Norwich, Martin has fallen from a ladder and has a nasty open ankle fracture

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits some of the restaurants that he set out to save, including the Mixing Bowl, the Olde Stone Mill, Peters and Campania. Have their owners pulled themselves out of their nightmares?

programme.title: Your Song
programme.desc: Alison Hammond presents the show giving people the chance to perform the one song that tells their story - often in front of a crowd for the very first time. Mentors Paloma Faith and Sam Ryder watch and listen to the performances. In the first episode, Alison heads to Liverpool, where a teenager sings Bocelli for the foster mum he cant find words to thank, and a kidney transplant recipient brings Sam and Paloma sprinting out into the downpour. With only one place available at the grand final at Londons Hackney Empire, Sam and Paloma face an impossible choice

programme.title: The Copenhagen Test
programme.desc: As Alexander races against the clock, Parker discovers a secret message hiding in plain sight. Espionage thriller, starring Simu Liu, Sinclair Daniel and Melissa Barrera

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Married couple Abbie and Charlie join host Anna Richardson and feature as the first picker pair as they search for another woman to join their relationship. Plus 41 year-old actor and glamour puss Chrissie from Leeds is hoping to find husband number four

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment
programme.desc: Brendon and Priya want to explore Priyas bisexuality, but it remains to be seen whether her steamy hot tub and sensual time in the bedroom will hit the spot. Chelsea and George disagree over how far they should have taken things in the bedroom with resident couple Kiki and Sophia

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to the upmarket quarter of Hollywood to help the owner of failing health-food restaurant Sante La Brea. As well as struggling to control his disrespectful staff, the proprietor receives a surprise visit from the police at the height of dinner service

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three pairs of partners in Lancashire take part in the dinner party competition, hoping to secure the £1,000 prize. First to host are Rosie and her husband David, who have been married for 25 years and are seriously loved-up. Next, young newlyweds Melisa and Kyle cook together for the first time to mixed reviews. The third and final evening is led by bickering couple Gary and Linda, but the duo may need to work hard if they want to be in with a chance of winning the cash prize

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien brings the fairground to Liverpool in the form of a strength-testing machine, while Henry Cole restores a childs racing car from a sideshow

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George tackles a cottage conundrum, as the previous owner has removed all the upstairs walls. The seasoned architect plans to create two more bedrooms and add a bit of privacy. Plus, he meets a couple who have plans to turn their loft into a cinema room

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: Ill Be Seeing You - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sam commissions a portrait of Diane, but the artist turns out to be so obnoxious that he forbids her to pose for him any more - an order she refuses to accept. Guest starring Christopher Lloyd

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: Ill Be Seeing You - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The pretentious Semenko begins to paint Diane, but Sam despises the artist so much that he soon orders Diane to stop posing for him

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part one of two. In the aftermath of their separation, Sam returns to his playboy ways and Diane dates her psychiatrist. Kelsey Grammer makes his first appearance as Frasier

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is shocked to discover his parents planned to divorce when he was a child, and only stayed together to help him recover from an accident. Tormented by the thought they have never really been happy together, he begins to blame himself for all the misery in their marriage

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra worries that Ray is happier at work than at home and persuades him to set up an office in the house, but things do not turn out as she planned

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra decides to have a go at writing a childrens book, but makes the mistake of asking Ray to help - little realising his competitive streak will jeopardise the entire project. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frederick wins a national spelling competition, but the other entrants parents claim Frasier helped him by silently mouthing the letters of difficult words - a view shared by the judges. Comedy, guest starring Trevor Einhorn

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Roz struggles to tell Alice her hamster is dead, while Niles and Frasier return to their childhood home and discover what they believe to be evidence of a murder

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Cam Winston and Frasier decide to suspend hostilities for the sake of their parents, who have supposedly fallen for each other - though their late-blossoming romance could just be a clever subterfuge. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: In Boston for a conference, Frasier and his family bump into many of his former Cheers cronies celebrating Cliffs retirement. Niles is overwhelmed with boredom, but Martin hits it off with Norm - an occasion that promises to cause more than a little embarrassment. Comedy, guest starring Rhea Perlman, George Wendt and John Ratzenberger

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the coastal community of Long Beach, California, to save an Italian restaurant that was opened in 1958 and now under the misguided leadership of Nino, who blamed by brother Michael and sister Carina for the eaterys failings. Their 78-year-old mother Inge desperate for Nino to turn the business around, but the feuding family members will have to solve their differences first

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien meet author and broadcaster Bob Flowerdew in Norfolk, where Henry eyes up a pinball machine from a damp shed

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The presenter and his parents explore the north of England by caravan. In the opening episode, they visit Lindisfarne, home to important monuments, heritage crafts and rare wildlife. After visiting the historic castle, the Bakers get a taste of island life at Lindisfarnes only distillery, and investigate the traditions behind its upturned herring boat huts. Janice helps out in a walled garden created by a trailblazing female horticulturist

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lee Juggurnauth helps retirees Marcia and Ron from Devon find a holiday home in Crete, Greece where they can take advantage of the climate, friendly people and beautiful beaches with a budget of £255,000

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Luxury holiday villa owners Paul and Helen host a group of tennis players and Kimmie and Matt hope for a busy summer full of bookings at their freshly finished gite renovation. On the Costa del Sol, Mike and Craig prepare for the busy season in their cafe

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: On the banks of the Lot river at Château de Cezerac, Jon and Carmen are giving their tired courtyard a full facelift. Comedy, starring Nicola Coughlan, Lydia West and Hannah Onslow, with a guest appearance from Rupert Everett

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The contestants get active as Armando Iannucci adopts a new persona to perform a daring stunt, Kumail Nanjiani grapples with bowling pins while wearing oven gloves and Joanna Page attempts a tricky skill she hasnt tried since she was about 12. Meanwhile, a topless Joel Dommett twirls a pretty umbrella and Amy Gledhill has to cope with someone shouting about scrambled eggs. Alex Horne keeps an eye on their progress, while Taskmaster Greg Davies hands out the points - and the put-downs when it all goes wrong

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Critically ill dialysis patient Gillian desperately needs fluids, so the East Anglian Air Ambulance crew must make a tough decision. In Canvey Island, the medics take over the breathing of motorcyclist Sol who is bleeding from his ear. In Luton, Essex and Herts Air Ambulance help 16-year-old Idris who has a head and leg injury after a collision with a car. In Norwich, Martin has fallen from a ladder and has a nasty open ankle fracture

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits some of the restaurants that he set out to save, including the Mixing Bowl, the Olde Stone Mill, Peters and Campania. Have their owners pulled themselves out of their nightmares?

programme.title: Your Song
programme.desc: Alison Hammond presents the show giving people the chance to perform the one song that tells their story - often in front of a crowd for the very first time. Mentors Paloma Faith and Sam Ryder watch and listen to the performances. In the first episode, Alison heads to Liverpool, where a teenager sings Bocelli for the foster mum he cant find words to thank, and a kidney transplant recipient brings Sam and Paloma sprinting out into the downpour. With only one place available at the grand final at Londons Hackney Empire, Sam and Paloma face an impossible choice

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton meets Andi and Neil from York, who dream of retiring permanently to Spain with their son Joel and spaniel Dixie. Theyre looking for a three-bedroom house with a dog-friendly garden, where barbeque enthusiast Neil can cook up a storm close to the beach close to the coastal town of Mazarron, near Murcia. Laura has five sizzling options for their budget of £145,000, but will any of them turn out to be their new forever home?

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Frank backs into Roberts patrol car, prompting Debra to ban him from driving the kids anywhere - and leaving Ray contemplating his dads advancing years. American comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra hires a babysitter - whose subsequent popularity with the children leaves her quietly seething with jealousy. To make matters worse, Marie feels insulted because she was not offered the job. Comedy, starring Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray fears Debra will seek revenge after he embarrasses her at a school auction. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.sub-title: The One with the Hypnosis Tape

programme.sub-title: The One with the Tiny T-Shirt

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey meets Miss Right, and Rachel is dismayed to learn her boss has started dating Chandler. Meanwhile, Monica inherits a dolls house, filling Phoebe with jealousy

programme.sub-title: The One with a Chick and a Duck

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey proves a hit with his co-star, despite what the critics think. Rachel dates a stranger who seems outwardly charming - until he starts screaming at people. Unfortunately, only Ross witnesses his bizarre behaviour and nobody else believes him. Guest starring Ben Stiller

programme.desc: Ross discovers a strange growth on his buttocks, Phoebe finds it hard to choose between two prospective lovers, and Monicas boyfriend surprises her. Comedy, starring David Schwimmer and Lisa Kudrow
