Elsewhere, Billy takes on jousting at Warwick Castle

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team transforms a home in Bolton, Greater Manchester for the Taylor-Mann family, making the house more suitable for nine-year-old William, who has severe autism. Williamss condition means that he is extremely hypersensitive to noise, and being in close proximity to his siblings is often just too much for him to bear. Nick Knowles and the team, with the help of kind volunteers, transform the property to create a quiet, calm space that can help William feel as comfortable as possible

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.sub-title: Terri, Esme and the Chi Family
programme.desc: Pete meets Lurcher Terri, whos terrified of the world and must learn to trust again, and helps settle a family of chihuahuas given up by their heartbroken owner

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: Gail Wright, Leeds General Infirmarys most experienced midwife, is on hand to help 22-year-old Keely when she goes into labour. Meanwhile, 29-year-old Steve tries to mask his anxiety with humour as he and fiancee Katie, 27, prepare for the arrival of their baby, who has the congenital condition gastroschisis - characterised by the intestines protruding through the abdomen wall - and will require surgery just hours after being born

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: The stories of three women preparing to give birth in Leeds maternity wards. Leahs partner is in prison and will miss the arrival of her sixth baby, while Becky and John have been to all the classes and know exactly what to expect. Sophie hasnt read anything about childbirth and has no idea what lies in store, but luckily partner Martins experience from a previous relationship enables him to provide much-needed support

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The newlyweds return from their honeymoon to a big welcome from both their families. However, Smithy seems disconcerted by his pals return and Nessa makes a confession that spoils the happy occasion. Comedy, starring Mathew Horne, James Corden and Joanna Page

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Smithy goes missing following Nessas shocking revelation, and the boys try to track him down to persuade him to accept his responsibilities. Meanwhile, Stacey is forced to contemplate the consequences of transforming her long-distance relationship with Gavin into a functioning marriage

programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: Its the Reading Festival and the junior doctors have no idea what?s in store for them, from a possible drug overdose to a mosh pit injury

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Bigger Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz Ashmawy presents the conclusion of the special focusing on rehousing Ukrainian refugees in six vacant terraced houses at Kingston College in Mitchelstown, North Cork. Baz is joined by Aoife Rhattigan and Kerry Hiddleston, who oversee the design, while Brandon Duarte from MMD Construction is in charge of the build, and Diarmuid Gavin plans the garden design

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Oscar-winning actress at her home in Surrey for a candid and lively conversation about her long career, her passion for theatre, her life away from acting and her relationship with her husband, the late Michael Williams. Louis also accompanies Judi to the Gielgud Theatre in Londons West End, where they discuss the difficulties of the lockdown and how it inadvertently led to her surprise popularity on TikTok

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: West Midlands paramedics assess a man police have found acting erratically on a busy dual carriageway and believe has taken the powerful psychoactive Monkey Dust

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Katie Piper: Locked Up in Louisiana
programme.desc: Katie meets Kimberly, who is accused of killing a woman she was dating, and learns that Kimberlys life derailed after she got into a car accident and became addicted to opioids

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Louis spends time with the global pop star Rita Ora. Louis meets Rita at her dads pub in West London where they sit down for a revealing conversation that takes in her rise to stardom, her familys arrival in the UK in 1990, and the difficulties she has faced dealing with intense press scrutiny and speculation about her personal life. Later, Louis spends time with Rita on the set of her latest music video in LA, before joining her as she puts on a spectacular live performance in Albania

programme.title: Deepfake Porn: Could You Be Next?
programme.desc: The rising trend of harmless images being turned into hardcore porn, footage that is often used against the people depicted with devastating consequences. The film features three women who have been impacted by this form of image-based sexual abuse, including a leading Florida State Senator, Lauren Book, the UK campaigner Kate Isaacs, and Dina, a woman who was deep-faked by a work colleague

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.sub-title: Nugget, Tetley and the Police Pups
programme.desc: Pete meets Chow Chow Nugget who has a longer stay than planned, three-legged Tetley, and some Belgium Malinois puppies training to be service dogs

programme.desc: Gail Wright, Leeds General Infirmarys most experienced midwife, is on hand to help 22-year-old Keely when she goes into labour. Meanwhile, 29-year-old Steve tries to mask his anxiety with humour as he and fiancee Katie, 27, prepare for the arrival of their baby, who has the congenital condition gastroschisis - characterised by the intestines protruding through the abdomen wall - and will require surgery just hours after being born

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: The stories of three women preparing to give birth in Leeds maternity wards. Leahs partner is in prison and will miss the arrival of her sixth baby, while Becky and John have been to all the classes and know exactly what to expect. Sophie hasnt read anything about childbirth and has no idea what lies in store, but luckily partner Martins experience from a previous relationship enables him to provide much-needed support

programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: Its a day of firsts for the junior doctors. Claire confronts her fears with a child patient, Tejas steps up to his first trauma case, and Adrian faces his first chest drain

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Smithy accompanies Nessa to the hospital for her first ultrasound scan, and the pair catch a glimpse of their baby. Stacey becomes unusually excited at the prospect of a weekend visit to her family home after she struggles to adjust to her role as an Essex wife - which proves to lack some of the glamour she had expected

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Bigger Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz Ashmawy presents the conclusion of the special focusing on rehousing Ukrainian refugees in six vacant terraced houses at Kingston College in Mitchelstown, North Cork. Baz is joined by Aoife Rhattigan and Kerry Hiddleston, who oversee the design, while Brandon Duarte from MMD Construction is in charge of the build, and Diarmuid Gavin plans the garden design

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Oscar-winning actress at her home in Surrey for a candid and lively conversation about her long career, her passion for theatre, her life away from acting and her relationship with her husband, the late Michael Williams. Louis also accompanies Judi to the Gielgud Theatre in Londons West End, where they discuss the difficulties of the lockdown and how it inadvertently led to her surprise popularity on TikTok

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: West Midlands paramedics assess a man police have found acting erratically on a busy dual carriageway and believe has taken the powerful psychoactive Monkey Dust

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team cannot believe their luck when a Volvo 144 rolls in with just 15,000 miles on the clock

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to North Wales to collect an Austin 7, and a gem of a Bentley, a British classic with coachwork built by top notch Parisian outfit Kellner who specialised in luxury

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien travel to East Sussex in their hunt to discover valuable items lost in peoples homes, with their finds including some Victorian street lamps

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: An examination of the dark history behind Britains fascination with murder, beginning with historian and curator Lucy Worsley exploring how notorious killings were transformed into popular entertainment in the first half of the 19th century

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the Severn to begin a trip along the south coast of Wales, joining maintenance workers on a hair-raising secret railway high above the estuary. From Newport, Michael makes for Caerleon, where he discovers how its Roman history was eclipsed by the legend of King Arthur and his Round Table

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his south Wales coastal railway journey in Barry Island, where he joins a Bristol pilot on his way to help guide a feeder container ship into port. Skirting the Glamorgan Heritage Coast, Michael alights at Llantwit Major to visit St Donats Castle, before reaching the pretty seaside resort of Porthcawl

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Port Talbot to Pembrey Burry Port
programme.desc: Michael Portillos coastal rail journey along the south coast of Wales reaches the town of Port Talbot, where he investigates how the steel industry is planning to decarbonise. Across the Bay, Michael arrives in The Mumbles, site of the worlds first passenger railway in 1807, before making his way from Gowerton to Burry Port and heading for the wild and blustery Pembrey Sands

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels the rural coastline of Carmarthenshire and Pembrokeshire in south Wales. From Carmarthen Bay, he tracks up the estuary and River Towy to the historic town of Carmarthen and Middleton Hall, home of Waless National Botanic Garden. There, Michael is on the trail of the quarter of a million honeybees that live in the garden

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul is off to the Lake District for a poignant collection from a Mathewsons regular - with seven vehicles to load including a Thames Truck, a Zodiac and a Vauxhall Victor

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks running late as usual, to collect a Triumph TR8 from Kirkby Stephen East Railway Station, with vintage engine ride thrown in for good measure

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Account of Operation Barbarossa, Germanys plan to invade the Soviet Union in 1941, revealing the massive human and financial cost endured by the Soviet people, who fought a bloody and lonely war with little support from the Allies. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi
programme.desc: A profile of Adolf Eichmann, the talented bureaucratic civil servant whose robotic willingness to follow orders led him to design the system that would bring about the holocaust

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second show this year from the Eden Project in Cornwall, where items include fragments of hair from the poet Samuel Taylor Coleridge, a set of beads given as a gift from a Zulu king to the Royal Navy surgeon who treated him and a Greek plate dating back to 300BC. Also, Marc Smith hears the stirring story of the Second World War pilot who shot down five enemy aircraft in a single night

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Sefton Park in Liverpool, where treasures include cufflinks made from 2,000-year-old coins, a letter from Kate Bush to one of her fans, a portrait from the English Civil War and an early version of the Beatles logo made for a drumkit. Fiona meets the descendent of a local hero, James Clarke, who moved to the city from Guyana at the turn of the 20th century and used his swimming skills to save countless lives in Liverpools docks

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The teams first purchase is a 1972 VW Fastback, and the second project sees them pick up a 40-year-old police bike

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team cannot believe their luck when a Volvo 144 rolls in with just 15,000 miles on the clock

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Account of Operation Barbarossa, Germanys plan to invade the Soviet Union in 1941, revealing the massive human and financial cost endured by the Soviet people, who fought a bloody and lonely war with little support from the Allies. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi
programme.desc: A profile of Adolf Eichmann, the talented bureaucratic civil servant whose robotic willingness to follow orders led him to design the system that would bring about the holocaust

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second show this year from the Eden Project in Cornwall, where items include fragments of hair from the poet Samuel Taylor Coleridge, a set of beads given as a gift from a Zulu king to the Royal Navy surgeon who treated him and a Greek plate dating back to 300BC. Also, Marc Smith hears the stirring story of the Second World War pilot who shot down five enemy aircraft in a single night

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon traces the path of the northern tropic around the globe, a journey in which he explores the environment, culture, history, politics and people of 18 countries. He begins in Baja California on Mexicos Pacific coast, crossing the countrys rugged interior, before heading east to Cuba and ending the first leg in the Bahamas

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Zak and Alex face a four-day mission to deliver a pair of classic steam locomotives for an Autumn Steam Gala at the Mid Hants Railway in Hampshire

programme.title: David Jasons Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the home from the lightbulb to the lawnmower, and discovers the role Queen Victoria played in creating the telephone

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The teams first purchase is a 1972 VW Fastback, and the second project sees them pick up a 40-year-old police bike

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A 1973 Ford Escort Mexico is the teams most expensive purchase to date. To save some cash Derek helps on their second buy - a 1968 Riley Kestrel

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Doncaster to collect four vintage BSAs motorbikes including one said to be the rarest BSA of all

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Experts Henry Cole and Simon OBrien travel Britain to find, fix and flog unwanted possessions on behalf of their owners, turning items into hard cash. A butter churn, a slot machine and a set of print trays are among the finds in this edition

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: In the second programme, the historian explores how science and detection influenced the popular culture of murder during Victorian times. Writers including Charles Dickens and Wilkie Collins were fascinated by grisly crimes, and the literary genre that came out of it captured the imagination of readers. The presenter also reveals that when Jack the Ripper began his reign of terror in London at the same time Robert Louis Stevensons Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde was published, the idea of the serial killer was introduced to the British public

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the most westerly station in Wales, on the final leg of his rail journey along its southern coastline. At Milford Haven, Michael learns from the Harbour Master about the biggest energy port in the United Kingdom, which has an oil refinery and storage terminals as well as natural gas terminals. Further along the estuary at Pembroke Dock, Michael visits a pioneering project to harness the power of the sea

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo returns to travel by train to more places around the British Isles, beginning by heading to the self-governing Isle of Man - a paradise for rail lovers. His first stop on the Manx Electric Railway is the capital of Douglas, where he reveals the islands parliament the Tynwald is older than Westminster, before the Isle of Man Steam Railway takes him down the east coast to Castletown, from where he heads to the Norse Castle Rusheword

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins this leg of his journey at Liverpools historic waterfront, where he visits the International Slavery Museum at Royal Albert Dock, before heading underground to discover one of the most important and secret rooms of the Second World War, the Royal Navys Western Approaches Headquarters. His next stop is Crosby on the Merseyside coastline in Sefton, before making his way north to Blackpool, the last stop on this leg of his journey

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey across the vast and beautiful Morecambe Bay, where he joins one of the towns last shrimpers to find out how potted shrimp is made, From there, Michael makes his way to Arnside on the Furness Line, where he discovers a magnificently quirky 17th century garden, and across the Arnside viaduct, he reaches Cartmel and its 12th century priory and a Michelin-starred restaurant

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave collects a barely used 1984 Honda Prelude from one of his all-time heroes - motorcycle legend, Ron Haslam

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jack and Amy are on a poignant trip to collect some motorbikes from a widow in the North East of England

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Bomber Command is unable to hit its targets precisely and attacks German cities under cover of darkness, while the Blitz in Britain brings public demand for revenge strikes, and Arthur Harris takes charge of Bomber Command

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi
programme.desc: A profile of Hermann Goering - the dashing, young, down-on-his-luck war veteran who turned into an extravagant, heartless, obese, drug-addled Nazi billionaire

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Powis Castle, one of the grandest houses in Wales, where treasures include a valuable oil painting depicting Scottish islands which was bought from a charity shop for £20, a 300-year-old ring crafted from a swans feather and woven with gold and a ventriloquists dummy. Plus, Runjeet Singh challenges Fiona to put three exquisitely designed ceremonial Indian weapons in order of value

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: To mark to coronation of King Charles, this special edition of the show goes back through the archives to celebrate items with a royal connection. Fiona Bruce is intrigued by a collection of coronation souvenirs from the past that have been preserved in the Museum of Brands, and ceramics expert Serhat Ahmet offers tips on the most collectable coronation keepsakes

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team fall for a Subaru Pick-up, which has all the Mathewsons in agreement. But will it all come crashing down when picking up parts proves tricky?

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A 1973 Ford Escort Mexico is the teams most expensive purchase to date. To save some cash Derek helps on their second buy - a 1968 Riley Kestrel

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Bomber Command is unable to hit its targets precisely and attacks German cities under cover of darkness, while the Blitz in Britain brings public demand for revenge strikes, and Arthur Harris takes charge of Bomber Command

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi
programme.desc: A profile of Hermann Goering - the dashing, young, down-on-his-luck war veteran who turned into an extravagant, heartless, obese, drug-addled Nazi billionaire

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: To mark to coronation of King Charles, this special edition of the show goes back through the archives to celebrate items with a royal connection. Fiona Bruce is intrigued by a collection of coronation souvenirs from the past that have been preserved in the Museum of Brands, and ceramics expert Serhat Ahmet offers tips on the most collectable coronation keepsakes

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon continues to trace the path of the northern tropic around the globe, joining travellers on the coast of Western Sahara to partake in the extreme sport of kite surfing, and meeting activists who campaign for indigenous peoples rights. He continues on through the worlds largest minefield, takes a gruelling train ride across Mauritania and visits huge refugee camps in the Algerian desert

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jacks baby Max makes his podium debut with Great Grandad Derek. Paul visits a former stock car racer and finds a Pinto pimped Anglia and a Talbot Sunbeam Lotus

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads north to pick up a 1954 Morgan +4 coupe in mint condition and believed to be just one of three still on the road

programme.title: Resto My Ride
programme.desc: Dec McKearney commits to building an EJ Holden for the EJ Whitten Charity Foundation, and with 9 weeks to finish, he has to call in his buddies for help

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team send their Paul Smith Mini in for a bit of designer tailoring by the team at Mini Sport

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek makes the short trip to a mysterious Yorkshire beauty spot to pick up a 1920s style bus that isnt all that it seems

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien repurposes some old suitcases, while Henry Cole gives an old dresser some serious bling

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian tells the story of one of the first high-profile killers - Dr Crippen, who was hanged in 1910 for poisoning and dismembering his wife - before turning her attention to the interwar period, when detective fiction reached the peak of its popularity at the hands of authors Agatha Christie and Dorothy L Sayers. After undergoing the elaborate initiation ceremony of the Detection Club, which was set up by a group of British writers in 1930, Lucy considers how Alfred Hitchcocks films and Graham Greenes books eclipsed the traditional murder-mystery story in the depiction of homicide

programme.desc: The historian tells the story of one of the first high-profile killers - Dr Crippen, who was hanged in 1910 for poisoning and dismembering his wife - before turning her attention to the interwar period, when detective fiction reached the peak of its popularity at the hands of authors Agatha Christie and Dorothy L Sayers. After undergoing the elaborate initiation ceremony of the Detection Club, which was set up by a group of British writers in 1930, Lucy considers how Alfred Hitchcocks films and Graham Greenes books eclipsed the traditional murder-mystery story in the depiction of homicide

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade visits Papua New Guinea, an island renowned for cannibalism, macabre rituals, headhunters and bitter tribal conflict. However, a new threat is sweeping through the region and he sets out on the trail of a creature that has reportedly been tearing chunks out of fishermen and devouring male body parts

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Eric Fogle is appointed as new head porter, putting Aluns nose out of joint. The hospital receives a letter of resignation from Dr Banner in Australia and has to appoint a partner to the practice - Weatherill believes she knows just the person, but Dr Ormerod favours Dr Burnett. Elsewhere, when Lucy Bayliss is involved in a horse-riding accident an X-ray reveals a more long-term problem. With Berwick Kaler and guest star Claire King

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the Olympic boxing silver medallist, who discusses his journey to the top of the amateur boxing world and his hidden struggles with addiction

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host as the remaining four famous faces compete in the semi-final as they take on healthy dishes. The two stars who fail to impress the judges will end up in the cook-off, with the pair fighting it out for the last place in the grand final

programme.desc: Archive news documentary chronicling the highs and lows of 2022 and all the chaos in between

programme.desc: Archive news documentary chronicling the highs and lows of 2023 and all the chaos in-between

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The boxer talks about his life inside and outside the ring. From growing up as a triplet to when he became the first Irishman to win an Olympic Gold medal in boxing in 1992

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul helps out with dogs who have a variety of issues, including a pair of old labradors that cannot bear to be apart for one second, a Staffy that hates being left alone and a tiny puppy with an anti-social behaviour problem

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The staff are worried about a Staffordshire bull terrier who is four times the size it should be, so Paul helps the dog try to lose some weight. He also meets a French bulldog who has been crippled by greedy breeders and a puggle called Bruce who is terrified of the world

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In London, three singletons go up against each other in a series of shopping challenges to win the affections of self-proclaimed princess Winter. To impress her, they have to dress her, but Winter has no information to go on but the outfits bought. Which of the three competitors will she choose for a prized first date?

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons try to impress dental nurse Cece in Cardiff, who then goes on a date with the person whose taste in clothes she liked best

programme.title: Lorraine Kelly: 40 Unforgettable Years
programme.desc: A tribute to the much-loved presenter and journalist who joined TV-am in October 1984, and became one of British TVs most popular broadcasters. Featuring archival footage from Lorraines early broadcasting career to the present day and interviews with those who know her best

programme.title: Whos Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Former Big Brother winner Josie Gibson puts on a dinner party at her home as she plays mystery host to four strangers. Presented by Brian McFadden

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade responds to reports from one of the US east coasts most popular vacation destinations, the sunny Florida Keys. A creature is attacking both visitors and locals alike, striking its victims so violently it is slicing open flesh and even smashing bones, and the assaults are happening both above and below the water. Jeremy is determined to discover the identity of the assailant and uncover what is triggering these attacks. He knows it is not going to be easy, and it is not long before the intrepid marine biologist fears he may be out of his depth

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: A profile of the Gaelic football legend who grew up in The Troubles in Armagh, and recovered from a missed penalty to score the winning goal in their only All-Ireland win

Can Paul and the Battersea team cheer up the newcomer?

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.sub-title: From the Grapefruit to the Skinny Gut Diet
programme.desc: Lauren and Robyn from Watford have 12 days to slim down before a 30th birthday party, with one eating half a grapefruit before each calorie-controlled meal and the other going on the apple cider vinegar diet. Two mums from north London want to lose weight for a friends daughters bat mitzvah in six weeks time, so Emma goes on a regimen to balance her gut bacteria, while Elisa takes on the blood-focused Eat Right for Your Type diet, and Carl and Paul in Wigan go on raw and fast food diets. programme.desc: In Beirut, Randall holds Professor Dawson while awaiting his paymasters instructions. Jean and Harry arrive in hot pursuit, each of them reeling from the messy end of personal relationships. Meanwhile, suspicious of Dalby, ultra-hawkish US General Cathcart asks CIA contact Maddox to begin a surveillance operation. His interest is rewarded when they find Dalby on a secret rendezvous in Beirut with Stok, a Russian Colonel. Stok has an offer - a dissident scientist to be exchanged for access to nuclear secrets. Dalbys initial dismissal falters as Stok reveals the dissidents identity

programme.title: Dublin After Dark
programme.desc: Efforts of members of An Garda Síochána to combat growing levels of anti-social behaviours on the streets of the capital. Garda Shauna Naughton and Garda Jun Han monitor the streets of Dublin on their nightly patrols in some of the worst hot spots for crime

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Satanic symbols painted on the church door convince the Reverend John Peggotty there is an evil presence in the churchyard, but George is certain it is the work of hooligans. However, an attack on Lord Ashfordlys godson Chas uncovers information that suggests a more personal motive, leading the investigation into the world of drugs and rock music. Elsewhere, Gina frets over some personal news. Guest starring Jeff Rawle, Adam Croasdell and Adam Kotz

programme.title: Titanic
programme.desc: Italian stoker Mario blags a job on the Titanic for his brother Paolo, who is instantly smitten with cabin steward Annie, and as the ship sets sail he startles her with an impulsive gesture. Barnes is surprised to find Watson loitering in steerage, while Mary lets her guard down with Peter Lubov, much to the anger of husband Jim. But any arguments are set aside when the ship strikes the iceberg - and fear quickly builds for the third-class passengers as some of them are behind locked gates

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A team of engineers are overhauling Londons underground infrastructure to build a world-class sewer system, and using cutting-edge tech and the latest construction methods, they work to transform a centuries-old network into a modern-day masterpiece

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: As the sun rises on the Costa Blanca, the Hotel Benidorm Plazas housekeeping staff spring into action. Meanwhile, expat Jessie has an audition to be the new hotel entertainer, and cameras follow the Parrot family as they head to Benidorm to spend some precious family time together

programme.title: My Unique BandB
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team visit Dorset, where they help rare-breed farmers Liam and Hannah to create a BandB and kitchen for a shepherds hut

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: Pat and Marek are reconciled, but when someone sees them together, their relationship is in more danger than ever. The Brindsleys come to realise the full extent of Davids injuries, and Teresa faces letting go of the past when she is asked out. Alison fears the consequences of her involvement with the Lyons, Laura is spurred into action by some happy news, and Joyce and Erica work together on a first aid meeting

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When mauled sheep carcasses are found, Nick and Kate suspect there may be a beast on the moors. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.sub-title: From a Mind-Body Cleanse to Frozen Belly Fat
programme.desc: Xand van Tulleken and Helen Lawal oversee proceedings as three more pairs road test some of the most popular and talked about diets. Sandra and Cherise hope to lose a dress size for a 30th birthday party, Dan and Rob from Stoke want to get fit for a charity football match and Pip and Donna have four months to slim down for a muddy obstacle race. Xand also investigates gastric balloon pills and whether he can freeze away his stomach fat

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi visits the Gyreum, an eco-lodge in Co Sligo, which is run by Colum Stapleton and his daughter Finn. Alex brings in a busload of eco-tourists to give their verdicts and discovers damp mattresses, cold showers and some questionable service, but sparks fly when she insists the owner needs to roll up his sleeves and fix the shortcomings

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary chronicling the highs and lows of 2022 and all the chaos in between

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary chronicling the highs and lows of 2023 and all the chaos in-between

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A piano teacher (guest star George Costigan) remains suspiciously secretive about his past when brought in to casualty after collapsing outside the hospital. Matrons revelation about Susie cools Aluns interest in her, and Mike takes pity on a mother while he treats her son. Elsewhere, Jill delivers bad news to an elderly patient and the porters are forced to take industrial action in support of a colleague

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a banks most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.sub-title: Benefit of the Doubt - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jack is targeted by a murderer as he continues his investigation into the mysterious death of an eminent surgeon - and it looks like the writing may be on the wall for Dentons foremost copper. David Jason, Bruce Alexander and Robert Glenister star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick finds himself drawn into a family feud after three children claim to have witnessed a supposed witch vandalising a funeral urn

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Frank Bailey is a pet detective whose methods irk some of the villagers, so when he is found dead in the kennel of a dog he was looking for, Barnaby has his work cut out for him. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Nick Hendrix and Fiona Dolman

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Todd finally speaks to the police to declare himself a victim of abuse, and Theo realises that his time is up when word of his violent and controlling behaviour starts to spread.

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bear makes a careless admission, while Jacob is uncomfortable and Lewis is intrigued

programme.title: Four Lives
programme.desc: Sarah Sak continues to press the police for information about Anthonys death but they are unable to even tell her when his body can be returned home to Hull. Following the discovery of another man, Gabriel Kovari, officers assume this is another overdose by a gay man and fail to investigate properly. Fact-based drama, starring Sheridan Smith and Stephen Merchant

programme.title: McDonald and Dodds
programme.sub-title: The Fall of the House of Crockett
programme.desc: Mystery drama set in Bath, where the wildly ambitious DCI McDonald finds herself paired with the shy, modest DS Dodds, who has languished on the shelf for most of his working life. However, it soon turns out the unassuming sergeant has a hidden talent for puzzle solving. Their first case sees the pair looking into the shooting of a homeless man in the deserted mansion of local industrialist Max Crockett. The Crockett appear to be a close-knit group but it soon becomes clear they have secrets to hide. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star as the detectives, with a guest appearance from Robert Lindsay

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: After Joes uniform goes missing and the police station is broken into, Geoff admits his Panda car was stolen the day before. George suspects an old adversary of deliberately stirring up trouble. Elsewhere, Rachel reassures a witness in a high profile case he is safe under her care, but a neighbour becomes suspicious of her visits

programme.title: Titanic
programme.desc: The epic drama reaches its conclusion. The ship hits the iceberg and panic takes hold. As the women and children board the lifeboats, Georgiana and Harry savour the few precious moments they have together, Barnes gives Watson a parting gift that will change her life for ever, and Louisa makes a surprising promise to Hugh. Below decks, Paolo tries desperately to save his brother and Jim searches for his daughter. As the liner is dragged underwater, the next few hours will decide who will live. And who will not. Linus Roache, Geraldine Somerville, Perdita Weeks, Lee Ross and Lyndsey Marshal star

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The history of modern-day mega structures, revealing that behind every seemingly impossible marvel of modern engineering is a cast of historic trailblazers

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Staff at Hotel Benidorm Plaza are trying to keep their cool as the reception air conditioning has a temporary malfunction, while guest experience manager Rosanna sweeps Holly and Dan off to one of Benidorms best-kept secrets. Owner Fede and his wife are turning 40 and have challenged hotel manager Angel to throw them a party at the hotel for the great and the good of Benidorm.

programme.title: My Unique BandB
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and the team are in Devon to help Faith create a unique BandB with a celestial theme as a tribute to her late father

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: The villagers prepare for harvest time, but Steph struggles to cope with the workload and faces losing the farm if she cannot meet her quota. Teresa faces a big decision, while Sarah finds herself in a dangerous situation. Alison discovers the real reason behind the parachute accidents and makes a horrifying connection to the factory. She races to warn Frances before it is too late

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick finds himself drawn into a family feud after three children claim to have witnessed a supposed witch vandalising a funeral urn

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well

programme.desc: Xand van Tulleken and Helen Lawal oversee proceedings as three more pairs road test some of the most popular and talked about diets. Jonno and Alfie have two weeks to slim down for a family barbecue, Sonal and Surekha go on six-week dieting plans to lose weight for an engagement party, and twins Michelle and Mandi want to trim some excess for a black-tie event in four months time. Xand also investigates investigates what happens when people are hungry and angry, and pits alcoholic drinks against one another to find out which is the worst for calories

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi visits The Richmoor Hotel in Weymouth, Dorset, where mother and manager Loraine employs several of her seven children. However, her mothering instincts have been getting in the way of business, and as a result, the service is suffering and the family-run hotel is on the verge of a revolt. programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirots help. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Part one of two. The team finds links between a 10-year-old murder and the discovery of a body in the local reservoir, prompting the irascible inspector to seek out one of the most ruthless and driven killers he has ever encountered. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander and Susan Penhaligon

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A new family cause panic in Aidensfield when their rabid dog goes missing, and Nick suspects Greengrass of poaching

programme.title: North Shore
programme.desc: Max meets an anonymous informant who hands over a key piece of evidence - only to flee before he can get any further answers. Meg is furious Max has acted alone and let a potential witness disappear. Abigail and Simon make plans to return Sophies body home - despite the British PMs orders for Abigail to finish negotiations on a trade deal between Australia and the UK

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah goes rummaging for evidence to aid Todds case against Theo, Ben and Eva cancel Maggies plans, Jodie takes an interest in Lilys plight, and another row with Carl triggers Debbies health

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Todd manipulates Manpreet, while April is struggling and Paddy is left reeling

programme.desc: Thanks to Ulysses cyber-infiltration, Lucie and her team are finally able to identify terrorists planning to carry out and attack in France. programme.title: The Widower
programme.desc: Factual drama based on the crimes of convicted murderer Malcolm Webster, co-written by Bafta winner Jeff Pope (Philomena). When mild-mannered nurse Malcolm (Reece Shearsmith) is confronted by his new wife over his wild spending, he starts to drug her to keep her quiet. However, a medical check-up then threatens to expose him, so he decides to silence his spouse once and for all. Three years on, he has a new bride - and history appears to be repeating itself. With Sheridan Smith and Kate Fleetwood

programme.desc: The chalk-and-cheese detective duo are called to a private clinic where a wealthy patient has been killed. programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines the case of Edith Negus from Bridlington, East Yorkshire. Edith died in October 2014 at the age of 102, but it was only after her death that her family discovered she was conned out of nearly £290,000 by Julie Sayles, her carer. Sayles bought two houses with money she took from a bank account she shared with Edith, and after being found out, was jailed for nine years in June 2017
